item.title: [Joule] Scalable fabrication of high-efficiency FAPbI3 perovskite solar modules in humid air via intermediate-phase engineering
item.description: By introducing 2-chlorobenzamidine acetate (CBAc) to form a stable PbI2·DMSO--CBAc intermediate, the hydration of PbI2·DMSO under high-humidity conditions is effectively inhibited, thereby achieving the controllable crystallization of phase-pure α-FAPbI₃ at room temperature. With this strategy, small-sized n-i-p and p-i-n PSCs, fabricated in humid air, achieved efficiencies of 26.06% and 26.52% (certified 26.29%), respectively. Moreover, slot-die-coated large-area modules (100.8 cm2) achieved 21.10% efficiency. This strategy shows great potential for large-scale and environment-compatible manufacturing.
item.author: Joule

item.title: [Nature Energy] Decoupling cation segregation and volatile loss in formamidinium–caesium metal halide perovskite solar cells under high-temperature operating conditions
item.description:
Nature Energy, Published online: 23 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02011-y

The stability of perovskite photovoltaics under thermal and light stress remains poorly understood. Wang et al. identify cation segregation and volatilization of formamidinium iodide as the primary degradation pathways at different temperatures.
item.author: Nature Energy

item.title: [Latest Results] Interfacial Coupling Design Enhancing Hole Transport in PTAA-Based Perovskite Solar Cells with Efficiency over 26%
item.description: Constructing 2D/3D perovskite heterojunction is an effective method to improve performance and stability of perovskite solar cells (PSCs), while the quantum well in 2D perovskites hinders carrier transport. To address this issue, π-conjugated semiconducting ligands have been introduced to enhance carrier-transfer capability of 2D perovskites. Here, two triphenylamine (TPA)-based ligands are specifically designed through π-extension with a fused (N-TPEAI) or covalently linked (P-TPEAI) benzene ring. For the first time, TPA semiconductor-based ligands have been incorporated to construct 2D/3D PSCs with poly[bis(4-phenyl)(2,4,6-trimethylphenyl)amine] (PTAA) as hole-transport materials (HTMs). Combined experimental and computational analyses reveal that this π-conjugation extension strategy proves to be effective in strengthening intermolecular interactions both between the adjacent spacer cations within 2D perovskites and at perovskite/PTAA interfaces, particularly in the case of P-TPEAI. Ultimately, the resultant 2D/3D PSCs employing P-TPEAI achieve an outstanding efficiency of 26.13%, which, to the best of our knowledge, is the highest value reported for 2D/3D PSCs incorporating PTAA HTMs. Moreover, benefiting from the robustness of both 2D perovskites and PTAA, the corresponding devices also exhibit excellent light-heat stability, meeting ISOS-L-2 protocol. These findings provide important guidelines for future design of organic spacers in advancing efficient and robust PSCs and related optoelectronic devices.
item.author: Latest Results

item.title: [Wiley: Advanced] Synergistic Passivation Enables 22.76%‐Efficiency Quasi‐2D Perovskite Solar Cells

item.title: [acs: Nano Lette] Dimerization of Organic Cations Triggered by Self-Interstitials Enhances the Stability and Carrier Lifetime of FAPbI3 Perovskites
item.description: Nano Letters, Volume 26, Issue 10, Page 3615-3623, March 18, 2026.
item.author: acs: Nano Letters: Table of Contents

item.title: [Joule] Stabilizing perovskite solar cells via facet-selective molecular engineering
item.description: This work demonstrates that tris(4-formylphenyl)amine (TFPA) selectively binds (100) facets in FAPbI3, directing growth to stable, face-on (111) facets and generating an extra polaron mode, which yields a 26.4% efficiency and exceptional operational stability.
item.author: Joule

item.title: [Wiley: Solar RR] Air Annealing‐Driven Preferential Growth of Low‐n Phases and Vertical Orientation in Cesium‐Based 2D Perovskite Solar Cells
item.description: Solar RRL, Volume 10, Issue 4, 26 February 2026.

item.title: [RSC - Energy En] In-situ Surface Matrix Solidification of FAPbI3 Perovskite Quantum Dots for Solar Cells with 19.37% Efficiency
item.description:
Energy Environ. Sci., 2026, Accepted Manuscript
DOI: 10.1039/D5EE07562A, Paper
Sicong Huang, Mingxu Zhang, Guoliang Wang, Zehong Yuan, Zhimei Sun, Xiaoliang Zhang
Formamidinium lead triiodide perovskite quantum dots (FAPbI3 PQDs) attract remarkable attention for new generation photovoltaics owing to their outstanding optoelectronic properties and solution processability. However, the surface matrix of PQDs...
The content of this RSS Feed (c) The Royal Society of Chemistry
item.author: RSC - Energy Environ. Sci. latest articles

item.title: [Joule] Tailored n value engineering of Dion-Jacobson 2D layers enables efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
item.description: This work shows that precise temperature control can tune the n value of a Dion-Jacobson two-dimensional perovskite interlayer, optimizing charge transport in 3D/2D perovskite solar cells. This approach simultaneously delivers a 25.56% power conversion efficiency with an n value-tailored 2D interlayer and remarkable operational stability and provides deeper insight into 3D/2D bilayer formation and degradation.
item.author: Joule

item.title: [Nature Communic] High Open-Circuit Voltage–Fill factor product in perovskite solar cells enabled by ferroelectric heterojunction modulation
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69391-3

Wu et al. report a strategy of incorporating either Dion-Jacobson phase or Ruddlesden-Popper phase ferroelectric perovskite with FAPbI3 to form bulk heterojunctions, increasing the open-circuit voltage-fill factor product and enabling single junction solar cells with an efficiency of 26.62%.
item.author: Nature Communications

item.title: [RSC - Energy En] Stabilizing Pure Phase of FAPbI₃ Perovskite Enabled by Solid-Liquid Low Entropy Ink
item.description:
Energy Environ. Sci., 2026, Accepted Manuscript
DOI: 10.1039/D5EE07451G, Paper
Kaiyu Wang, Guang Yang, Yuan Jiang, Han Qin, Jianbing Zhu, Xueqin Ran, Yingdong Xia, Lingfeng Chao, Xiaozheng Duan, Yonghua Chen
Phase-pure α-formamidinium lead iodide (α-FAPbI₃) perovskites that exclude additional cations or anions (for example, methylammonium (MA⁺), Cs⁺, or Br⁻) hold great promise for high-efficiency and thermally stable perovskite solar cells....
The content of this RSS Feed (c) The Royal Society of Chemistry
item.author: RSC - Energy Environ. Sci. latest articles

item.title: [Latest Results] Enabling Highly Efficient and Stable Perovskite Photovoltaics via A Multidentate Molecular Anchor Additive
item.description: Suppressing formamidinium (FA) loss and perovskite phase degradation is very crucial for achieving highly efficient and long-term stable perovskite solar cells (PSCs). Herein, we designed and synthesized a novel multifunctional additive (ZL1) to stabilize α-FAPbI3 perovskite phase through synergistic multisite interactions: i its F atoms form F···H–N hydrogen bonds with FA+, (ii) its phenyl rings participate in cation–π interactions with FA+, (iii) the C=O and S groups coordinate Pb2+ through Lewis acid–base interactions, and (iv) the NH groups engage I− anions through N–H···I hydrogen bonding. Consequently, ZL1 molecule can effectively suppress FA loss and optimizes perovskite crystallization kinetics, yielding high-quality and stable α-FAPbI3 perovskite films with enlarged grain sizes and reduced defect density. Meanwhile, ZL1 treatment promotes exciton dissociation, facilitates hole extraction from the perovskite layer into the hole transport layer, and reduces charge carrier recombination in device. The ZL1-modified device achieves a power conversion efficiency of 26.13%, significantly outperforming the control device (24.20%). A similar improvement is observed in wide-bandgap PSCs, with efficiency increasing from 18.44% to 20.53% after ZL1 treatment. Notably, the unencapsulated ZL1-based devices exhibit exceptional operational stability under both illumination and thermal conditions.
item.author: Latest Results

item.title: [Latest Results] One-Step Formation of 2D/3D Perovskite Heterojunction via Ligand Intercalation and Facet Engineering for Efficient Perovskite Solar Cells
item.description: Two-dimensional/three-dimensional (2D/3D) perovskite heterojunctions at the contact interfaces have been proven to enhance the stability and power conversion efficiency (PCE) of perovskite solar cells (PSCs). The 2D/3D bilayer is typically formed via a solution post-treatment onto the 3D perovskite, where the 2D layer’s dimensionality depends on the ligand size and its reactivity. Despite their stability, long-chain ligands typically form 2D perovskites with low dimensionality (n = 1, 2) which feature poor charge conductivity and mobility. Here, we propose an in situ fabrication method incorporating long-chain oleylammonium (OlyA+) ligands directly into the perovskite ink. This approach forms 2D perovskite with higher dimensionalities (n ≥ 3) with enhanced (001) crystal facet orientation of the 3D film, improved energetic alignment, charge extraction, and structural stability. The fabricated inverted PSCs with 1.55 eV bandgap achieved a maximum PCE of 26.22% for small area and 24.6% for 1cm2 devices, as well as 21.1% for mini-modules (6.8 cm2). Additionally, the PSCs with in situ formed 2D/3D perovskite heterojunctions retained 90% and 80% of their initial PCE after 1200 h photothermal stability and 1050 h outdoor testing, respectively. Our one-step strategy produces uniform and stable 2D/3D perovskite heterojunctions with enhanced passivation capability, overcoming the limitations of conventional sequential methods and offering a promising and effective approach for highly stable and efficient PSCs.
item.author: Latest Results

item.title: [Wiley: Advanced] Dual‐Mode Molecular Regulation of Perovskite Crystallization Enables Efficient and Stable FAPbI3 Solar Cells and Modules

item.title: [Nature Energy] Buried 2D/3D heterojunction in n–i–p perovskite solar cells through solid-state ligand-exchange reaction
item.description:
Nature Energy, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-01980-4

Two-dimensional perovskite phases at the bottom interfaces of solar cells improve performance, but their formation is challenging. To address this, Zhao et al. graft the 2D perovskite-phase precursor onto a functionalized SnO2 electrode substrate.

item.title: [Joule] Stabilizing α-FAPbI3 perovskite via centered formamidinium cation immobilization
item.description: Pyrene at perovskite grain boundaries immobilizes formamidinium cations, stabilizing the PbI6 cage and rigidifying α-FAPbI3. Rigid α-FAPbI3 exhibited a uniform spatioenergetic landscape, reduced electron-phonon coupling, facilitated efficient charge transport, and suppressed perovskite degradation. Pyrene-incorporated α-FAPbI3 retained its photoactive phase with over 25% power conversion efficiency and operational stability exceeding 3,400 h.
item.author: Joule

item.title: [RSC - Energy En] Chemical bridging in a 2D/3D heterojunction via dual-anchoring functionalized molecules for efficient, stable and flexible perovskite solar cells
item.description:
Energy Environ. Sci., 2026, Advance Article
DOI: 10.1039/D5EE03302K, Paper
Lei Zhao, Jie Zhang, Yikun Hua, Xinyue Song, Chao Wu, Ruiqian Chen, Yang Feng, Haoran Deng, Jiacheng Su, Lei Gu, Wei Hui, Weiyuan Chen, Chunming Yang, Lin Song
Me-TACl modification of the upper interface constructs stable 2D perovskites and forms strong 2D/3D interface coupling, enabling the preparation of high-performance perovskite solar cells with one stone and two birds.
To cite this article before page numbers are assigned, use the DOI form of citation above.

item.title: [RSC - Energy En] Blocking multifaceted degradation pathways via fluorinated hydrogen-bond armor for stable perovskite solar cells
item.description:
Energy Environ. Sci., 2026, Advance Article
DOI: 10.1039/D5EE04336K, Paper
Yilin Gao, Yuehui Li, Guozhen Liu, Naitian Zhang, Siao Li, Zhiyong Wang, Zheng Lv, Yinjuan Zhang, Lin Yang, Jie Zhang, Linghui Zhang, Wenrui Li, Wenming Tian, Wenzhe Li, Zhehan Ying, Jijun Qiu, Yantao Shi
The fluorinated hydrogen-bond armor strategy was proposed to comprehensively protect formamidinium cations from deprotonation. The device achieved high efficiency of 26.51% and exceptional durability under ISOS-L-2 and ISOS-D-2 protocols.
To cite this article before page numbers are assigned, use the DOI form of citation above.
